response.evidence: Hispanic/Latino applicants show consistently lower acceptance rates across demographic groups compared to non-Hispanic counterparts with similar loan profiles. Hispanic females: 77.78% acceptance vs non-Hispanic females 88.37% (Asian), 94.17% (American Indian), 92.86% (Pacific Islander), 93.49% (White). Hispanic males: 86.11% acceptance vs non-Hispanic males 89.74% (2+ races), 92.38% (American Indian), 92.16% (Asian), 85.89% (Black), 88.76% (Pacific Islander), 93.60% (White). Hispanic applicants also tend to have comparable or slightly better loan-to-value and debt-to-income ratios, suggesting loan quality is not materially different. The consistent pattern of lower approval rates for Hispanic applicants across all racial categories suggests potential discrimination in the approval process.

Hispanic or Latino applicants show consistently lower acceptance rates compared to non-Hispanic or Latino applicants across multiple demographic groups. Hispanic Latino acceptance rates range from 77.78% to 92.16%, while non-Hispanic Latino rates range from 88.37% to 93.59%. The disparity is most pronounced among Female applicants (77.78% vs 88.37% for 2+ minority races; 90.41% vs 86.48% for Black applicants) and among White Male applicants (90.51% vs 93.60%). Additionally, Hispanic Latino applicants across most groups show higher average debt-to-income ratios (44-45%) compared to their non-Hispanic counterparts, yet receive fewer loan approvals despite similar loan-to-value ratios. This pattern suggests systemic disparities in approval outcomes based on ethnicity, independent of age.

Latino applicants (Hispanic or Latino derived_ethnicity) show systematically lower acceptance rates compared to non-Latino applicants across most demographic groups. Hispanic Latino acceptance rates range from 77.8% to 92.2%, while Non-Hispanic Latino rates range from 83.1% to 94.2%. Most strikingly, White Male Hispanic Latino applicants have 90.5% acceptance vs White Male Non-Hispanic Latino at 94.0%. Asian Male Hispanic Latino shows 92.2% vs 93.1% for Non-Hispanic. Black/African American Male Hispanic shows 88.9% vs 85.9% for Non-Hispanic (exception). This pattern is consistent across Female groups as well. The 1-4 percentage point acceptance rate gaps, while appearing modest, are consistent and systematic across racial/ethnic categories, suggesting potential discrimination in loan approval processes against Latino applicants.

Among Hispanic or Latino applicants across all racial categories, acceptance rates range from 77.78% to 94.0%, with a median of approximately 88.89%. This is comparable to or exceeds acceptance rates for non-Hispanic applicants in many demographic groups. For example: Asian Hispanic males (92.16%), White Hispanic males (90.51%), and American Indian Hispanic males (83.14%) show acceptance rates within the overall distribution. Non-Hispanic groups show rates of 88.37% to 93.61%. The data does not show a systematic pattern of lower acceptance rates for Hispanic applicants relative to similarly situated non-Hispanic applicants within the same racial category. Loan characteristics (amounts, debt-to-income ratios, loan-to-value ratios) are also similar across Hispanic and non-Hispanic groups, suggesting comparable lending standards.

Hispanic or Latino applicants show systematically lower acceptance rates across almost all demographic categories compared to non-Hispanic counterparts. Hispanic females: 77.8% vs non-Hispanic females 88.4% (Asian), 94.2% (American Indian), 90.4% (Black), 89.9% (White). Hispanic males: 86.1% vs non-Hispanic males 89.7% (2+ races), 92.4% (American Indian), 92.2% (Asian), 88.9% (Black), 90.5% (White). The pattern is consistent across all racial groups. Additionally, Hispanic applicants tend to have lower average incomes and property values while maintaining similar debt-to-income ratios, suggesting potential systematic barriers in approval despite comparable financial profiles.

Latino applicants show systematically lower acceptance rates compared to non-Latino counterparts across multiple demographic groups. Latino females: 77.8% vs non-Latino females 88.4% (American Indian), 94.2% (Asian). Latino males: 86.1% vs non-Latino males 89.7% (2+ minority races), 92.4% (American Indian), 92.2% (Asian), 88.9% (Black), 89.7% (Native Hawaiian), 90.5% (White). The pattern is consistent across all racial categories. Additionally, Latino applicants often have lower average loan amounts and property values despite similar or lower loan-to-value and debt-to-income ratios, suggesting potential approval barriers. The acceptance rate disparity ranges from 6-15 percentage points lower for Latino applicants, representing meaningful differential treatment in the lending process.

Latino applicants show systematically lower acceptance rates compared to non-Latino applicants across most racial categories. Latino acceptance rates range from 77.8% to 92.2%, while non-Latino acceptance rates range from 83.1% to 94.2%. Most notably: Latino females average 77.8-91.1% vs non-Latino females at 88.4-94.2%; Latino males average 80.6-92.2% vs non-Latino males at 85.9-93.6%. This pattern is consistent across all racial groups (Asian, White, Black, Native Hawaiian, American Indian). Latino applicants also tend to have slightly higher average debt-to-income ratios (43.7-45.4%) compared to non-Latino applicants (43.6-44.3%), yet receive lower approval rates, suggesting ethnicity may be a factor independent of financial metrics. However, the differences are not uniformly extreme (some overlap exists), and loan amount/property value/income differences could partially explain approval rate variations.

Latino applicants (Hispanic or Latino ethnicity) show consistently lower acceptance rates compared to non-Latino applicants across multiple demographic groups. Latino acceptance rates range from 77.8% to 92.2%, while non-Latino acceptance rates for the same race/sex combinations range from 88.4% to 94.2%. For example: Latino females (2+ races: 77.8% vs 88.4%), Latino males (2+ races: 86.1% vs 89.7%, Asian: 92.2% vs 93.1%, White: 90.5% vs 93.6%). The pattern is consistent across race and sex categories, suggesting systematic lower approval rates for Latino applicants. Additionally, Latino applicants often have lower average loan amounts and property values despite similar debt-to-income ratios, suggesting they may face stricter lending criteria.

Latino applicants (Hispanic or Latino ethnicity) show systematically lower acceptance rates compared to non-Latino applicants across most demographic groups. Latino acceptance rates range from 77.78% to 92.16%, while non-Latino acceptance rates range from 83.14% to 94.17%. Notably, Latino males show 86.11% acceptance vs 92.38% for non-Latino males in the American Indian category, and Latino males show 90.51% vs 93.60% for non-Latino males in the White category. Additionally, Latino applicants receive slightly lower average loan amounts and have marginally higher debt-to-income ratios on average, suggesting potentially more conservative lending toward this group. The pattern is consistent across race/sex categories, indicating ethnicity-based disparities in approval likelihood.

Latino applicants (Hispanic or Latino ethnicity) show systematically lower acceptance rates compared to non-Latino applicants across most racial categories. Hispanic/Latino acceptance rates range from 77.8% to 86.1%, while Non-Hispanic/Latino rates range from 88.4% to 93.5%. For example: Asian Female Hispanic (94.0%) vs Non-Hispanic (93.1%), White Female Hispanic (89.9%) vs Non-Hispanic (93.5%), White Male Hispanic (90.5%) vs Non-Hispanic (93.6%), and Black Male Hispanic (88.9%) vs Non-Hispanic (85.9% exception). Additionally, Hispanic/Latino applicants across categories have lower average loan amounts and property values, suggesting they may be offered smaller loans or approved for lower-value properties, which could indicate discriminatory lending practices.
